verses.text: Stephen begins, 'The God of glory appeared unto our father Abraham,' calling him out. From the first. He traces God's grace from the very beginning.
Stephen begins, 'The God of glory appeared unto our father Abraham,' calling him out. From the first. He traces God's grace from the very beginning.
Stephen begins, 'The God of glory appeared unto our father Abraham,' calling him out. From the first. He traces God's grace from the very beginning.
God brought him to this land and gave the covenant of circumcision. Faithful promise. God kept His word through the patriarchs.
God brought him to this land and gave the covenant of circumcision. Faithful promise. God kept His word through the patriarchs.
God brought him to this land and gave the covenant of circumcision. Faithful promise. God kept His word through the patriarchs.
God brought him to this land and gave the covenant of circumcision. Faithful promise. God kept His word through the patriarchs.
God brought him to this land and gave the covenant of circumcision. Faithful promise. God kept His word through the patriarchs.
The patriarchs sold Joseph, but 'God was with him,' making him governor of Egypt. Overruled malice. God turned their envy to deliverance.
The patriarchs sold Joseph, but 'God was with him,' making him governor of Egypt. Overruled malice. God turned their envy to deliverance.
A famine came; Jacob's sons went down to Egypt for corn. Providence. God used hunger to gather the family.
A famine came; Jacob's sons went down to Egypt for corn. Providence. God used hunger to gather the family.
A famine came; Jacob's sons went down to Egypt for corn. Providence. God used hunger to gather the family.
Jacob came down, and they died and were buried. End of an age. The fathers passed, but the promise lived on.
Jacob came down, and they died and were buried. End of an age. The fathers passed, but the promise lived on.
Jacob came down, and they died and were buried. End of an age. The fathers passed, but the promise lived on.
Israel grew; a new king arose who oppressed them. Trial deepens. The promise advances through affliction.
Israel grew; a new king arose who oppressed them. Trial deepens. The promise advances through affliction.
Israel grew; a new king arose who oppressed them. Trial deepens. The promise advances through affliction.
Moses was born, learned 'in all the wisdom of the Egyptians,' mighty in word and deed. Prepared deliverer. God shapes His servant long before his work.
Moses was born, learned 'in all the wisdom of the Egyptians,' mighty in word and deed. Prepared deliverer. God shapes His servant long before his work.
Moses was born, learned 'in all the wisdom of the Egyptians,' mighty in word and deed. Prepared deliverer. God shapes His servant long before his work.
Moses slew an Egyptian and tried to reconcile his brethren. Too soon. He acted before God's appointed time.
Moses slew an Egyptian and tried to reconcile his brethren. Too soon. He acted before God's appointed time.
Moses slew an Egyptian and tried to reconcile his brethren. Too soon. He acted before God's appointed time.
Moses slew an Egyptian and tried to reconcile his brethren. Too soon. He acted before God's appointed time.
Rejected, 'Who made thee a ruler?' Moses fled to Midian. Spurned leader. Israel refused the very one God would send.
Rejected, 'Who made thee a ruler?' Moses fled to Midian. Spurned leader. Israel refused the very one God would send.
Rejected, 'Who made thee a ruler?' Moses fled to Midian. Spurned leader. Israel refused the very one God would send.
In the bush God called him to deliver Israel out of Egypt. The call. God commissions His servant in His own hour.
In the bush God called him to deliver Israel out of Egypt. The call. God commissions His servant in His own hour.
In the bush God called him to deliver Israel out of Egypt. The call. God commissions His servant in His own hour.
In the bush God called him to deliver Israel out of Egypt. The call. God commissions His servant in His own hour.
In the bush God called him to deliver Israel out of Egypt. The call. God commissions His servant in His own hour.
This Moses, whom they refused, God sent as ruler; he foretold 'a prophet... like unto me.' Pattern of Christ. The rejected deliverer points to the rejected Saviour.
This Moses, whom they refused, God sent as ruler; he foretold 'a prophet... like unto me.' Pattern of Christ. The rejected deliverer points to the rejected Saviour.
This Moses, whom they refused, God sent as ruler; he foretold 'a prophet... like unto me.' Pattern of Christ. The rejected deliverer points to the rejected Saviour.
In the wilderness they would not obey, but turned back to Egypt in heart. Rebellion. They despised the law and the leader.
In the wilderness they would not obey, but turned back to Egypt in heart. Rebellion. They despised the law and the leader.
In the wilderness they would not obey, but turned back to Egypt in heart. Rebellion. They despised the law and the leader.
They made a calf and worshipped the host of heaven; God 'gave them up.' Idolatry judged. Persistent sin meets God's withdrawal.
They made a calf and worshipped the host of heaven; God 'gave them up.' Idolatry judged. Persistent sin meets God's withdrawal.
They made a calf and worshipped the host of heaven; God 'gave them up.' Idolatry judged. Persistent sin meets God's withdrawal.
They had the tabernacle; David found favour, and Solomon built the temple. God's dwelling. Worship had its place by God's appointment.
They had the tabernacle; David found favour, and Solomon built the temple. God's dwelling. Worship had its place by God's appointment.
They had the tabernacle; David found favour, and Solomon built the temple. God's dwelling. Worship had its place by God's appointment.
They had the tabernacle; David found favour, and Solomon built the temple. God's dwelling. Worship had its place by God's appointment.
Yet 'the most High dwelleth not in temples made with hands'; heaven is His throne. Beyond walls. God is not confined to any building.
Yet 'the most High dwelleth not in temples made with hands'; heaven is His throne. Beyond walls. God is not confined to any building.
Yet 'the most High dwelleth not in temples made with hands'; heaven is His throne. Beyond walls. God is not confined to any building.
Ye stiffnecked, 'ye do always resist the Holy Ghost'; ye slew the prophets and betrayed the Just One. Bold rebuke. He turns their own history against them.
Ye stiffnecked, 'ye do always resist the Holy Ghost'; ye slew the prophets and betrayed the Just One. Bold rebuke. He turns their own history against them.
Ye stiffnecked, 'ye do always resist the Holy Ghost'; ye slew the prophets and betrayed the Just One. Bold rebuke. He turns their own history against them.
They gnash on him; he sees the glory of God, and 'Jesus standing on the right hand of God.' Open heaven. The dying martyr beholds his Lord.
They gnash on him; he sees the glory of God, and 'Jesus standing on the right hand of God.' Open heaven. The dying martyr beholds his Lord.
They gnash on him; he sees the glory of God, and 'Jesus standing on the right hand of God.' Open heaven. The dying martyr beholds his Lord.
They stone him; he prays, 'Lord Jesus, receive my spirit... lay not this sin to their charge,' and falls asleep. Christlike death. He forgives his murderers as his Master did.
They stone him; he prays, 'Lord Jesus, receive my spirit... lay not this sin to their charge,' and falls asleep. Christlike death. He forgives his murderers as his Master did.
They stone him; he prays, 'Lord Jesus, receive my spirit... lay not this sin to their charge,' and falls asleep. Christlike death. He forgives his murderers as his Master did.
